name: HBA_alpha_globin_cluster_chr16p13_3
description: Alpha-globin gene cluster on 16p13.3, containing HBZ - HBA2 - HBA1 - HBQ1 in tandem (~30 kb). HBA1 and HBA2 are >99% identical and lie in two ~4 kb directly-oriented duplication blocks; NAHR between them produces the canonical alpha-thalassaemia deletions (-alpha3.7, -alpha4.2).

architecture.internal_structure: 5' HS-40 (regulatory) - HBZ (zeta) - HBA2 (alpha-2) - HBA1 (alpha-1) - HBQ1 (theta-1) 3'; HBA1/HBA2 in ~4 kb directly oriented SD blocks (-alpha3.7, -alpha4.2 NAHR products); high paralog identity drives recurrent CNV.
